description_long: This object appears to be a heavily damaged or partially destroyed airplane, with a central fuselage section and remnants of wings and tail structure. The fuselage is boxy and metallic, with visible torn and jagged edges, suggesting a crash or severe disassembly. The surface is primarily a dull, weathered gray with patches of brown and beige, possibly from rust, dirt, or exposed internal components. Some mechanical and structural elements, such as wiring, tubing, and fragmented panels, are visible protruding from the sides. The wings are mostly missing or broken, with only stubs or fragments remaining attached to the fuselage. The overall condition is derelict, with no clear cockpit, engines, or landing gear visible, and the object is surrounded by scattered debris.
description: A wrecked, metallic airplane fuselage with jagged edges, missing wings, and exposed internal components, showing signs of severe damage and decay.
description_view_0: The front or main side of the airplane shows the boxy fuselage with torn edges and some attached debris, highlighting the damaged structure.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the depth of the fuselage and the extent of missing or broken components, with some internal parts visible.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the fuselage is shown, with more exposed internal elements and jagged, broken surfaces.
description_view_3: A profile view emphasizes the thinness of the remaining structure and the lack of intact wings or tail, with debris scattered nearby.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, broken wing stubs and no visible dihedral or winglets.
attribute_engine_type: No engines visible; engine mounts or nacelles are absent or destroyed.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail configuration with remnants of a single vertical stabilizer; horizontal stabilizers are broken or missing.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No landing gear visible; possible remnants of fixed gear mounts.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit area, heavily damaged and not clearly enclosed; no canopy or windows visible.
